taskDescription: Click on all the muscles of the superficial layer of the anterior forearm mentioned in the text below.
textField: The anterior forearm has muscles arranged in superficial, intermediate, and deep layers. The superficial layer consists of four muscles originating from the medial epicondyle via the common flexor origin. *Pronator* *teres* pronates the forearm and the median nerve passes between its two heads. *Flexor* *carpi* *radialis* flexes and abducts the wrist, and its tendon is the landmark for the radial artery pulse. *Palmaris* *longus* weakly flexes the wrist and is absent in about 15 percent of people. *Flexor* *carpi* *ulnaris* is the only superficial flexor supplied by the ulnar nerve rather than the median nerve. The intermediate layer contains flexor digitorum superficialis, while the deep layer contains flexor digitorum profundus, flexor pollicis longus, and pronator quadratus.
